goal: Complete production platform readiness through four merge-gated phases.

phases.title: Finite platform capability contract
acceptance_criteria: A finite four-phase roadmap is committed and schema validated.
Every known limitation has one closure phase and an existing evidence file.
Completed engine and analyzer scopes are excluded.
Current API health and container entrypoints are inventoried without changing runtime behavior.
A dedicated pull-request workflow validates this roadmap.

phases.title: Account and login-state closure
acceptance_criteria: Administrator bootstrap requires explicit production configuration.
Login state expires can be revoked and remains valid across supported process restarts.
Browser cookie attributes and state-changing request boundaries are explicit.
Login and registration include deterministic abuse controls.
Identity and authorization boundaries have API regression coverage.

phases.title: Durable state and job lifecycle closure
acceptance_criteria: User feedback and login state persistence is atomic and integrity checked.
Every job has owner timestamps terminal state and immutable artifact manifest.
Retention user quota and global disk budget cleanup are deterministic.
Restart recovery separates complete incomplete and damaged jobs.
Remote persistence failure is observable and cannot replace newer local state.

phases.title: Operations and deployment closure
acceptance_criteria: Beta live and maintenance modes are explicit and consistently enforced.
Liveness and readiness return stable machine-readable checks.
Requests and jobs have correlation identifiers and structured lifecycle logs.
The production container passes non-root health shutdown and resource smoke tests.
Deployment is gated on mandatory CI and an auditable source revision.
